meta.title: AI Datacenter Buildout — Where Value Accrues
meta.subtitle: Thematic review

slots.title: AI Datacenter Buildout
slots.subtitle: Where value accrues across the chain

layers.name: Accelerators
layers.role: Training + inference compute; pricing power at the bleeding edge

layers.name: Networking
layers.role: Scale-out fabric; share shifting to Ethernet

layers.name: Power & cooling
layers.role: Grid interconnects and liquid cooling as new bottleneck

layers.name: Hyperscale operators
layers.role: Capex deployers; capture value only if AI revenue follows

takeaway.heading: Takeaway
takeaway.body: Scarcity sits **upstream** — packaging and accelerators. Operators carry the capex risk.

companies.name: Nvidia
companies.mechanism: Accelerator ASP × volume; near-monopoly at training

companies.name: Vertiv
companies.mechanism: Thermal + power per rack rises with density

companies.name: Arista
companies.mechanism: Ethernet share gains in AI fabrics

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.mechanism: AI revenue must outrun depreciation of buildout

takeaway.heading: Takeaway
takeaway.body: High narrative + low profit sensitivity (MSFT) is where disappointment risk concentrates; VRT keeps the best ratio of earnings torque to narrative crowding.

commentary.heading: Trend
commentary.body: Uptrend intact above the 200-day; momentum constructive but extended.

commentary.heading: Risk
commentary.body: Crowded long; a capex digestion headline is the obvious de-rating trigger.

commentary.heading: Read-through
commentary.body: Aggregate capex still accelerating — supports the upstream scarcity thesis for **2–3 more quarters**. The thesis breaker is two consecutive guides of decelerating capex.
